mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held below its trailing 12-month average signals an accommodative monetary stance that lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as capital rotates into risk assets.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.04 in June 2017, near multi-year lows despite gradual hikes
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend deviation and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or accommodative periods do not show statistically higher mean returns than restrictive periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, it signals an inflection in the labor cycle that historically precedes broad earnings deceleration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.3-4.4% in mid-2017, low but historically a turning-point zone
failure_condition: In triggered instances, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the mean triggered return is not significantly different from the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) indicates a joint contraction in consumer and housing demand that is not visible from either series alone, and this combined weakness leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as forward earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM decline AND HOUST MoM decline concurrently
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised_value 465130 vs vintage 473513, HOUST revised_value 1157 vs vintage 1092 showing divergence in mid-2017 vintages
failure_condition: Joint-decline signal fails if forward 20d returns following simultaneous declines are not more negative than returns following single-series declines alone, tested via difference-in-means with p > 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, decelerating INDPRO growth (industrial production) signals capacity constraints and margin compression for manufacturers, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; in early-cycle expansions the same deceleration is not predictive because it reflects normalization rather than exhaustion.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage_value 105.16 vs revised 100.3471, indicating a long expansion phase by mid-2017
failure_condition: Hypothesis rejected if INDPRO deceleration in late-cycle windows (identified via NBER-style expansion age proxies) shows no significant negative relationship with 60d forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) across two or more distinct late-cycle episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in PAYEMS (nonfarm payrolls) reflects continued labor market expansion that supports near-term consumer spending and corporate revenue, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS revised_value 146583 vs vintage 146404, consistent monthly gains in 2017
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward return following positive PAYEMS prints is not significantly greater than following flat/negative prints (p > 0.05) across a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above its trailing 6-month moving average by more than 25 basis points, it signals an accelerating tightening cycle that triggers short-term risk-off repositioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following trading week before markets reprice the terminal rate.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above trailing 6-month moving average by > 25bp
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.04 in June 2017 amid a gradual but persistent hiking cycle since Dec 2015
failure_condition: If triggered crossings show positive or neutral 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances, or the effect does not persist across at least two distinct tightening episodes, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a decline in HOUST (housing starts) signals amplified rate sensitivity in the housing sector that spills over into broader equity risk sentiment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month; in an easing cycle the same decline in HOUST is not predictive because falling rates offset housing weakness.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised_value 1157 vs vintage 1092 amid ongoing Fed hikes through mid-2017
failure_condition: Hypothesis rejected if HOUST declines during tightening cycles are not associated with significantly more negative 20d forward returns than HOUST declines during easing cycles (interaction term p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in CPIAUCSL (inflation) above trend combined with stagnant or declining INDPRO growth signals an emerging stagflationary condition that compresses equity valuations through margin pressure and discount-rate uncertainty, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; neither series alone captures this joint supply-cost squeeze.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5% AND INDPRO MoM growth <= 0
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL revised_value 244.163 vs vintage 243.79 with INDPRO showing large vintage-to-revision divergence in mid-2017 data
failure_condition: Rejected if joint-trigger periods do not produce significantly more negative 60d forward returns than periods with only high CPIAUCSL or only low INDPRO individually (interaction effect p > 0.05), or if fewer than 3 historical instances of the joint trigger can be identified for validation.
